threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' like it’s the only line that matters. Everyone knows Dalston's a bus ride away from adventures.
Caught a guy on the 37 last night who brought his sax and played smooth jazz. Bus went quiet, even the usual drunks stopped to listen. Just London, innit?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a saxophone echoing from the courtyard while kids played and an old lady fed pigeons. That's Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can dance on a rooftop in Kreuzberg during a festival, drink a beer, and listen to a 90s techno DJ while fireworks go off. Unique vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know that the best tacos are hidden in a taco truck on 16th, not in the fancy spots on Valencia. It’s a game changer.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 tonight, and a guy was shouting Shakespeare while balancing on one leg. Only in the Tenderloin, right? Muni’s the real stage.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think all tapas are the same; they miss how each bar has its own secret recipes and that bar staff can be quite territorial about them.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they keep looking at the metro map like it’s a treasure map—confusion mixed with hope, especially at the Passeig de Gràcia stop.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep stepping off the Overground at the wrong exits, still think 'the Gospel' means a Sunday service, not a local bar.
Last week on the 59, a guy started singing opera. Everyone was silent, then joined in. Brixton magic at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard a neighbor's cat meowing along to a street musician's guitar on the corner of Weserstraße. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find a kebab shop in Kreuzberg that's open 24/7 and serves a special vegan döner with curry sauce. Only in Berlin will you taste that combo.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their nonchalant reaction to the sight of a guy juggling fire while waiting for the 14. Tourists stare, we just keep scrolling.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, I watched a guy in a tuxedo serenade the bus driver with a ukulele as we waited at Market. Only in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all about Gaudí. They miss the real heart in the smaller plazas, where locals gather and actually live.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their struggle to pronounce 'Gràcia' correctly—it's not 'Gracia' like in Spanish, but with the Catalan twist. It's endearing.
